content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: General | Workplace/Place of Duty (lieu de travail)] the choice to renew with his current lender or switch to another lender for the remaining balance. Payments are made on a fixed schedule and involve payments of principal and interest. For greater clarity, a mortgage does not include any loan which allows the borrower to access funds for other purposes, such as Home Equity Lines of Credit. New principal residence (nouvelle résidence principale) - a single-family dwelling purchased or rented at the new workplace which will become the employee’s principal residence following the relocation. Non-Accountable Incidental Expenses Allowance (Indemnité pour les faux frais non soumis à une justification) - an allowance that employees do not have to account for upon relocation i.e. receipts are not required but should be retained. Pet (animal de compagnie) - any animal that normally resides in the family home and is kept for companionship (if the animal is allowed in the living room, it can be moved). Large animals such as horses or sheep or large numbers of domestic animals, such as a cattery, or a kennel of dogs, are not considered pets. Personal Declaration (déclaration) – means a written statement signed by the employee attesting to and listing the expenses for payment without receipt. Personal Motor Vehicule (PMV) [véhicule automobile personnel - (VAP)] - for purposes of shipment, means a sedan, sports car, station wagon, mini van, pick-up, or 4-wheel drive vehicle of three-quarter ton rating or less, registered in the employee’s name or in the name of the spouse or common-law partner, or a dependant, the primary purpose of which is for family conveyance. This definition excludes racing cars, campers, and any other vehicle which does not meet the above criteria. Principal residence (résidence principale) - a single-family dwelling owned or rented and occupied by the employee or dependant residing with the employee, which was occupied continuously at the time the relocation at public expense was authorized and which is recorded as the employee’s permanent address on the departmental or agency personnel file. Temporary or seasonal accommodation is excluded from this definition. Receipt (reçu) - an original document or electronic/paper copy showing the date and amount of expenditure paid by the employee. Relocation (réinstallation) - the authorized move of an employee from one workplace to another. The move consists of the relocation from the principal residence occupied while working at the old workplace, to the new principal residence that will be occupied

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: General

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: General | Workplace/Place of Duty (lieu de travail)] the employee’s permanent address on the departmental or agency personnel file. Temporary or seasonal accommodation is excluded from this definition. Receipt (reçu) - an original document or electronic/paper copy showing the date and amount of expenditure paid by the employee. Relocation (réinstallation) - the authorized move of an employee from one workplace to another. The move consists of the relocation from the principal residence occupied while working at the old workplace, to the new principal residence that will be occupied while working at the new workplace. For greater clarity, the employee must relocate to a new principal residence to be eligible for the reimbursement of relocation expenses. Single-family dwelling (habitation unifamiliale) - living quarters containing the normal amenities necessary for continuous year-round occupancy. The dwelling must be structurally separate and have an entrance or entrances from outside the building or from a common hall, lobby, vestibule, or stairway inside the building. Spouse or common-law partner (conjoint ou conjoint de fait) - the term “common- law partner” refers to a person living in a conjugal relationship with an employee for a continuous period of at least one year. The term “spouse” refers to the person married to the employee. Third-party service provider rates and fees (taux et frais des fournisseurs de service tiers) - contracted ceiling amount charged by the Contracted Relocation Service Provider to the Government of Canada for providing third-party real-estate, legal, building inspection and rental assistance services. Transferee (personne transférée) - the employee being relocated from one place of residence to a new destination to perform duties of a position within the public service. Travelling expenses (frais de voyage) - the transportation and living expenses incurred en route to the new workplace. Permanent/Regular (permanent/régulier) - the single permanent location determined by the employer at, or from which, an employee ordinarily performs the work of his or her position or reports to; (a) the workplace of employees who are employed as Ships’ Officers or Ships’ Crews shall be deemed to be a suitable structure, as determined by the employer: (b) at the location of the home port of their vessels, for employees whose normal duties are performed aboard a vessel; or (i)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.1 Effective Date

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.1 Effective Date] 1.1.1 This Directive comes into effect January 1, 2021.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.1 The purpose of this directive is to ensure fair treatment of employees

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.1 The purpose of this directive is to ensure fair treatment of employees] authorized by the employer to relocate to a new principal residence at a new regular workplace consistent with the principles above. The aim is to relocate an employee in the most efficient fashion, at the most reasonable cost to the public while having a minimal detrimental effect on the employee and his/her family and on departmental operations.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.2 This directive provides a personalized approach for each participant’s particular

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.2 This directive provides a personalized approach for each participant’s particular] needs. It has two facets:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.3 This directive will ensure access to professional relocation services advice at

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.3 This directive will ensure access to professional relocation services advice at] every step of the employee’s move. An employee makes the final decision to accept or reject professional advice provided. at the location where employees normally perform their duties when they are not employed on a vessel. (ii) a policy formula that marries direct reimbursement of expenses, over which the employee has little control, and an individualized approach to benefits providing an opportunity for the employee to select what is best for him/her (within a given Fund) given his/her own family or unique circumstances; and (a) a contract with a CRSP who will provide the employee with professional assistance at every step of the relocation with the view of presenting the employee with every reasonable opportunity to maximize the available benefits. This includes relocation planning, marketing assistance and destination services along with several other enhanced relocation services. (b)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.4 This directive and any limitations thereto are published as policy and not as

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.4 This directive and any limitations thereto are published as policy and not as] permissive guidelines. Discretion, be it at the employee, managerial or departmental level, shall be confined to those provisions where discretion is specifically authorized.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.5 Relocation expenses must be directly attributable to the relocation, and must

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.5 Relocation expenses must be directly attributable to the relocation, and must] be clearly reasonable and justifiable. They must not upgrade the financial position of the employee and must be supported by receipts as stipulated within this directive. The provisions shall provide only for the employee’s legitimate expenses, without opening the way for personal gain or for the underwriting of extravagances.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.6 Entitlements not identified within this directive will not normally be reimbursed

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.6 Entitlements not identified within this directive will not normally be reimbursed] unless deemed to result from exceptional circumstances. Deputy head or delegated departmental authority is required for payment of such expenses.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.7 Employees requesting reimbursement of entitlements not identified within this

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.7 Employees requesting reimbursement of entitlements not identified within this] directive must provide a written submission to the Departmental National Coordinator (DNC) who shall then forward the submission with its recommendation to the deputy head or delegated departmental authority for approval.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.8 The applicable rates and allowances contained in the NJC Travel Directive, as

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.8 The applicable rates and allowances contained in the NJC Travel Directive, as] modified from time to time by the NJC, shall form part of this directive unless otherwise stated within this directive. They shall be applied to all eligible participants irrespective of age, sex, marital or family status, or disability.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.9 Travel and shipment of household goods and effects (HG&E) provisions shall

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.2.9 Travel and shipment of household goods and effects (HG&E) provisions shall] comply with the provisions of the current NJC Travel Directive and the Household Goods Removal Service contract, unless otherwise specified in this directive.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.1 Inquiries concerning the shipment of personal and household effects and

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.1 Inquiries concerning the shipment of personal and household effects and] automobiles should be directed to the Central Removal Service (CRS).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.2 Inquiries concerning commercial travel and accommodation reservations

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.2 Inquiries concerning commercial travel and accommodation reservations] should be directed to the Government of Canada Travel Service Supplier. 1.3.3 Inquiries concerning tax should be directed to Canada Revenue Agency (CRA).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.4 Inquiries regarding the provisions of this directive should be directed to

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.3.4 Inquiries regarding the provisions of this directive should be directed to] Departmental National Coordinators (DNC).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4 Application

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4 Application] 1.4.1 This directive applies to employees who work in: 1.4.2 Subject to section 2.1 Authorization, this directive applies to: Note: Where the assignment and/or term is for less than three (3) years, the provision on sale and purchase of property will not apply (see section 13.5).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.3 The 40 km rule: should the relocation not meet the 40 km rule, relocation

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.3 The 40 km rule: should the relocation not meet the 40 km rule, relocation] benefits may be taxable.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.4 Normally, relocation shall only be authorized when the employee’s new

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.4 Normally, relocation shall only be authorized when the employee’s new] principal residence is at least 40 km (by the shortest usual public route) closer to the new place of work than his/her previous residence, in accordance with subsection 248(1) of the Income Tax Act.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.5 The formula for calculating the distance can be found in Appendix A (CRA’s form
metadata.subsection: Schedules I and IV of the Financial Administration Act; and

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.5 The formula for calculating the distance can be found in Appendix A (CRA’s form | Schedules I and IV of the Financial Administration Act; and] T1-M E (99) “Claim for Moving Expenses.”). 1.4.6 This directive does not apply to: all departments and other portions of the public service of Canada listed in (a) organizations listed in Schedule V of the Financial Administration Act that are members of the NJC and that have opted to follow this directive. (b) term employees appointed to indeterminate positions;(a) term employees whose period of employment is to be longer than one (1) year;(b) employees on assignment of more than one (1) year’s duration;(c) employees on Leave Without Pay (LWOP) for less than one (1) year; or(d) employees on priority status in accordance with the Public Service Employment Act (PSEA) and the Public Service Employment Regulations (PSER) or employer- specific priority status policies. (e) employees whose relocation is governed by the Foreign Service Directives or other authorities (e.g. the RCMP, Canadian Armed Forces); or (a)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.7 Public service organizations not referred to in subsection 1.4.1 of this directive

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.4.7 Public service organizations not referred to in subsection 1.4.1 of this directive] may request to be included under this directive by the Program Authority at Treasury Board Secretariat.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 1.5.1 This directive only applies to relocations which were authorized on or

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 1.5.1 This directive only applies to relocations which were authorized on or] after January 1, 2021. Relocations authorized prior to this date are subject to the provisions contained in the NJC Relocation Directive, effective April 1, 2009.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.1 When the employer has determined that relocation is required, the employer

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.1 When the employer has determined that relocation is required, the employer] shall authorize the relocation and ensure that all relocation arrangements are consistent with the provisions of this directive. The Departmental National Coordinator (DNC), specifically identified for this program, is required to issue approval in certain areas of this directive, on behalf of the deputy head.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.2 The authorization shall be in advance in writing. A relocation is considered

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.2 The authorization shall be in advance in writing. A relocation is considered] authorized when the relocation file has been successfully registered by the employer with the CRSP. The employee shall not incur any expenses under this directive until the initial consultation with the CRSP has taken place. This authorization shall be provided at least 30 days prior to the date the employee must report at the new workplace, as stated in the relevant staffing document, unless a shorter period is personally authorized by the deputy head or the delegated departmental authority.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.3 The employer shall authorize the CRSP in writing to provide contracted

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.3 The employer shall authorize the CRSP in writing to provide contracted] relocation services to the employee referred.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.4 The CRSP is not authorized to reimburse any expenses that are not covered by

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.4 The CRSP is not authorized to reimburse any expenses that are not covered by] this directive, or expenses that are over and above the established contractual rates of this directive. employees on educational or developmental leave within Canada and employees on assignment or interchange within and outside the public service for periods of less than one (1) year. (b)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.5 In exceptional circumstances, the Departmental National Coordinator (DNC), on

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.5 In exceptional circumstances, the Departmental National Coordinator (DNC), on] behalf of the deputy head, may post authorize relocation expenses incurred up to 30 days prior to the date of registration with the CRSP. Unless authorized under this section, the employer shall not be responsible for expenses incurred prior to authorization of the relocation.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.6 The employer shall not be responsible for such expenses, unless and until the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.1.6 The employer shall not be responsible for such expenses, unless and until the | Responsibilities] relocation is subsequently authorized and the employee may be ineligible for some entitlements.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2 Employer Responsibilities

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2 Employer Responsibilities] 2.2.1 The employer shall:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.2 On behalf of the employer, DNCs specifically identified for this program, who

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.2 On behalf of the employer, DNCs specifically identified for this program, who] are required to issue approvals in certain areas of this directive, shall: ensure that this directive is available to relocating employees at the employees’ normal workplace during the employee’s working hours; (a) determine that a relocation is necessary;(b) register the relocation with the CRSP;(c) advise the employee being relocated not to proceed with any relocation-related activities prior to initial consultation with the CRSP; (d) provide the relocating employee with the necessary leave to carry out all activities related to the relocation; (e) allow flexibility of reporting dates to enable the employee to coordinate relocation activities effectively and in the most cost-effective manner; (f) ensure employees are reimbursed for actual and reasonable relocation expenses (via the CRSP) within the limits of this directive; (g) initiate the removal of household effects, by forwarding to CRS a completed "Requisition for Removal Services"; and (h) ensure that accommodation of employee needs is provided to the point of undue hardship. (i) 2.2.3 On behalf of the employer, the CRSP shall:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.4 The employer shall, in consultation with the CRSP, initiate the removal of

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.4 The employer shall, in consultation with the CRSP, initiate the removal of] household effects, by forwarding to CRS a completed “Requisition for Removal Services”. CRS will provide the employee with a copy of “Removal Instructions”, which provide a detailed summary of the employee’s and the mover’s responsibilities concerning the removal of household effects. be familiar with both this directive and other directives as required;(a) recommend, interpret, approve, process payment, review and audit expense claims/entitlements; (b) work closely with departmental authorities to ensure that:(c) no inappropriate funding/relocation commitments are made to employees; (i) the correct pre-authorization process is followed; and(ii) no employee starts a relocation without having had the contracted counselling from the CRSP; (iii) provide instructions and/or advice in writing, either through the CRSP or directly, when requested by an employee; and (d) provide the CRSP with all relevant information to assist in the coordination of the employee's move and to minimize the time spent in interim accommodation. (e) provide services as specified in the contract and in this directive;(a) establish contact with the referred employee within 48 hours and confirm personal information and counselling dates as per the contract; (b) ensure that the timing of the relocation, and the travelling associated with it is planned to minimize disruptions to family life, and to minimize the costs to the employer; (c) verify and approve relocation expense claims before reimbursement;(d) advance funds to the employee immediately prior to the house-hunting trip or the movement of HG&E; and (e) process claims as soon as possible after receiving fully completed expense claims with supporting documentation. (f)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.5 The employer shall provide the relocating employee and spouse or common-

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.2.5 The employer shall provide the relocating employee and spouse or common-] law partner, if applicable, with the necessary leave to carry out all activities related to the relocation.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.3 Employee Responsibilities

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.3 Employee Responsibilities] 2.3.1 The employee shall: 2.3.2 The employee should be aware that: become familiar with the provisions of this directive and consult with the CRSP prior to engaging in any relocation-related activities; (a) inform the employer or its suppliers of needs that may require accommodation;(b) follow instructions and/or advice on the relocation process, given by the CRSP;(c) require that the instruction and/or advice be provided in writing when the employee feels that it contradicts this directive; (d) consult with the DNC for clarification on the instruction and/or advice provided, when necessary; (e) ensure that an “arm’s length” relationship exists when selecting service providers; (f) ensure that any rebate from the service provider be paid to the Receiver General of Canada, if the Government of Canada pays for the fees charged for those services; (g) submit within 90 days after the date of the employee’s arrival at the new workplace, or the date the dependant(s) arrive(s), whichever is later, a complete relocation expense claim with necessary supporting documentation as required by this directive; and (h) make full restitution immediately upon notification to the CRSP, at the end of the relocation process, if he or she has received funds that should not have been provided. (i) proceeding with relocation related transactions prior to authorization or incurring expenses beyond those allowable under this directive could result in such expenses not being reimbursed; (a) refusing the instruction and/or advice of the DNC, when this instruction and/or advice does not contradict this directive, could result in relocation problems and (b)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.3.3 Employees who are in the process of dissolving a marriage or spousal union, or

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.3.3 Employees who are in the process of dissolving a marriage or spousal union, or] who are in a situation of indefinite separation that may result in dissolution must inform the CRSP and provide confirmation of separation in the form of a declaration or legal separation agreement.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.4.1 User departments/agencies are to assume the program delivery costs in

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.4.1 User departments/agencies are to assume the program delivery costs in] accordance with the approved fee-for-service schedule.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.4.2 All requests for reimbursement that fall within the intent of this directive, but

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.4.2 All requests for reimbursement that fall within the intent of this directive, but] whose special circumstances have not been specifically addressed, must be forwarded through normal channels directly to the designated DNC at the head office.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.5 Forms and Files

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.5 Forms and Files] 2.5.1 Specific directions shall be incorporated in departmental procedures as follows:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.6.1 Where reimbursement for authorized expenses is sought, the employee is

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.6.1 Where reimbursement for authorized expenses is sought, the employee is] expected to submit receipts for the expenses incurred. additional personal cost; and eligibility for some or all the provisions of this directive may be forfeited if he/she signs contracts (realtor, lawyer, appraisers, pre-sale, etc.), or has been reimbursed directly by the employer for relocation related expenses. (c) the specific relocation provisions arranged for an employee-requested relocation for personal reasons (see subsection 2.3.3) shall be placed on the employee's relocation file; reimbursement shall be confined to the terms agreed to, and shall be subject to the limitations of this directive; (a) the pre-authorization to travel "Travel Authority and Advance" form, shall be completed prior to the move and placed on the employee's relocation file; and (b) when an isolated post is not involved, a record of all time extensions authorized, and of long-term storage approved shall be placed on the employee's relocation file. (c)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.6.2 In exceptional circumstances, where the employee certifies that a receipt was

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.6.2 In exceptional circumstances, where the employee certifies that a receipt was] lost, accidentally destroyed or unobtainable, a personal declaration may replace the receipt.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.7.1 If an employee with a spouse or common-law partner is relocated, and the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.7.1 If an employee with a spouse or common-law partner is relocated, and the] spouse or common-law partner is also an employee who is relocated to the same location within one year of the registration of the file, this directive shall apply as for an employee and spouse or common-law partner and not as two separate employees. 2.7.2 If an employee with a spouse or common-law partner is authorized to relocate, and the spouse or common-law partner (who is not an employee) expects to receive relocation benefits from their employer, the employee must choose either to receive relocation benefits through their spouse’s or common-law partner’s employer, or through this Relocation Directive; the employee may not claim both. This choice must be made within 15 working days from the employee’s receipt of notification of the registration of the file and before any relocation expenses are incurred.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.7.3 Time-off with pay shall also be granted to the spouse or common-law partner

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.7.3 Time-off with pay shall also be granted to the spouse or common-law partner] who is also an employee.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.8.1 An employee shall be advanced funds (via the CRSP) to assist in meeting

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.8.1 An employee shall be advanced funds (via the CRSP) to assist in meeting] personal expenses incurred in the relocation such as a house hunting trip (HHT), travel and interim accommodation.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.8.2 To ensure that the employee has full use of the proceeds of the sale of the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.8.2 To ensure that the employee has full use of the proceeds of the sale of the] former residence, the employee may also request an advance of an amount equal to the calculated real estate and legal fees. This amount may be issued directly to the legal firm, provided that a written offer of purchase has been received and subject to the residence being listed through a licensed real estate firm. Such advances may be made in accordance with the Accountable Advances Regulations, and shall only be made immediately prior to the date the funds will be required.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.9.1 Employer-requested relocations are relocations within Canada, including

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.9.1 Employer-requested relocations are relocations within Canada, including] employee relocations that result from staffing actions except on initial appointment.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.10.1 In an “employee-requested relocation” assistance shall be provided in

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.10.1 In an “employee-requested relocation” assistance shall be provided in] accordance with Part XII.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.11.1 Relocation provisions for appointees to the public service or other persons

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.11.1 Relocation provisions for appointees to the public service or other persons] who are not employees before they are authorized to relocate at public expense are found in the Initial Appointees Relocation Program (IARP), which can be found on the Treasury Board Secretariat website.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.12.1 An employee claiming relocation expenses must submit a detailed and

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.12.1 An employee claiming relocation expenses must submit a detailed and] itemized account, in the form required. The claim must conform to the following requirements:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.1 In accordance with section 2.1 Authorization, the employer shall reimburse an

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.1 In accordance with section 2.1 Authorization, the employer shall reimburse an] employee for expenses incurred when a relocation is cancelled by the employer for work-related reasons that are beyond the employee’s control in compliance with this directive. the account must be submitted within 90 days after the date of the employee’s arrival at the new workplace, or the date the dependant(s) arrive(s), whichever is later [but within one (1) year]; (a) the claim shall be supported by receipted vouchers for each item in the claim except: kilometric allowance, expenses for taxis under $12, expenses for meals and miscellaneous incidental expenses, and amounts paid for accommodation other than in commercial establishments; (b) the claim shall be supported by any other information required; and(c) the claim shall be signed or digitally signed by the employee, certifying that all the amounts claimed have been paid. (d) 2.13.2 Upon official notification of cancellation, it is the responsibility of the employee to terminate any arrangements in process except for the removal of effects or the move of a mobile home, which the employer will terminate through the CRS.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.3 An employee who wishes to cancel a relocation prior to the household goods

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.3 An employee who wishes to cancel a relocation prior to the household goods] and effects being moved must request approval to cancel the relocation from the DNC. The DNC may approve the cancellation of the relocation if the DNC is satisfied cancellation is appropriate in the circumstances. Once the household goods and effects have been loaded the relocation may not be cancelled at the employee’s request. 2.13.4 Reimbursable expenses will vary according to the stage of the relocation. Reimbursable expenses are those which in the opinion of the deputy head or delegated departmental authority, are reasonable in the circumstances, not exceeding the limits prescribed in this directive. Normally, little expense is involved until the employee disposes of a principal residence. For example, an employee renting accommodation may have terminated the lease and may be unable to retain the accommodation; or an employee owning a principal residence may have sold it and may be obliged to vacate. In the latter case, although the employee may be able to have the sale set aside by agreement with the purchaser, a real estate fee may be payable. In either case, when the employer is satisfied that the employee must vacate the accommodation, a local move of household effects shall be approved, along with incidental relocation expenses in the appropriate amount.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.5 The employer will reimburse an employee for a wide range of incidental

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.5 The employer will reimburse an employee for a wide range of incidental] expenses related to a relocation. The expenses must be directly attributable to the move, must be clearly reasonable and justifiable, and must not upgrade the financial position of the employee.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.6 The incidental expenses must be supported by receipts, the reimbursement of

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.6 The incidental expenses must be supported by receipts, the reimbursement of] which is subject to judgment by the recommending authority that the expenses are within the intent of the provision and are reasonable in the circumstances.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.7 Reimbursement of payments made for rent in advance [see Temporary Dual

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.7 Reimbursement of payments made for rent in advance [see Temporary Dual] Residence Assistance (TDRA)] of the move, or for legal fees on purchase of a principal residence, may also be approved.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.8 If the employee’s employment with the Government of Canada terminates for

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.13.8 If the employee’s employment with the Government of Canada terminates for] any reason during the relocation, the expenses incurred following the date of termination of employment are not reimbursable under this directive.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.14.1 If an employee is authorized to relocate while in travel status at the new

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.14.1 If an employee is authorized to relocate while in travel status at the new] workplace, such circumstances shall change the status from travel to relocation. If the employee is eligible for assistance such as TDRA, it shall be approved. The assistance shall start the day following the effective date of written relocation notification.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 2.15.1 There is a one (1) year time limit to receive reimbursement on a relocation

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 2.15.1 There is a one (1) year time limit to receive reimbursement on a relocation] from the date of registration with the CRSP. Based on exceptional circumstances, employees may be granted an extension of up to three months by submitting a request to the Departmental National Coordinator (DNC).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.1.1 This directive provides a customized approach for each participant’s particular

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.1.1 This directive provides a customized approach for each participant’s particular] needs. It applies to eligible employees relocating within Canada. It provides employees with access to professional relocation services such as planning, marketing assistance and destination services throughout every step of the employee’s move.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.1.2 This directive is designed to encourage and facilitate door-to-door moves

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.1.2 This directive is designed to encourage and facilitate door-to-door moves] thereby eliminating the need for additional assistance such as TDRA, etc., through improved move management.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.2.1 This directive is divided into two separate yet interdependent components: Core

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.2.1 This directive is divided into two separate yet interdependent components: Core] Fund and Personalized Fund. The Core Fund is further divided into two components, the Basic Core and the Core Customized. Entitlements listed in this section are explained in more detail in other sections of the Directive.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.1 The purpose of this section is to describe the Basic Core entitlements available

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.1 The purpose of this section is to describe the Basic Core entitlements available] to employees who are relocating. It includes those basic provisions covering the reimbursement of eligible expenses, such as real estate commissions and legal fees, which are directly reimbursed by the employer via its CRSP, and includes some enhancements such as relocation planning and destination services.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.2 Basic Core entitlements within described parameters are 100% funded by the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.2 Basic Core entitlements within described parameters are 100% funded by the | Core Customized Fund] department unless specifically stated otherwise. For example, an employee who incurs real estate commission expenses for $15,000 would be reimbursed the $15,000. 3.3.3 For information on the conditions and limitations of particular entitlements, refer to the appropriate section of the Directive. The following is an overview of the entitlements available to relocating employees under the Basic Core Fund: Long-term storage (isolated posts only) mortgage breaking penalties - non- portable mortgages only Mortgage interest differential on purchase (non-portable mortgage) 3.3.4 While the utilization of the Basic Core Fund of this directive is not mandatory, there is no provision under any circumstances for those items that are not used, to be exchanged or assigned any monetary value which could be added to the Core Customized Fund of the Directive. The employee who chooses not to use the Basic Core Fund entitlements forfeits them. For example, an employee who opts not to go on a HHT foregoes a Core entitlement and will not receive a monetary value for this action.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.5 The Core Customized Fund includes items that can be reimbursed, up to the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.5 The Core Customized Fund includes items that can be reimbursed, up to the] value calculated from pre-budgeted amounts within the Core Customized Fund entitlements.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.6 This fund is provided by the employer to allow employees to claim other

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.6 This fund is provided by the employer to allow employees to claim other] elements of a move that are not covered under the Basic Core Fund. It provides the flexibility to choose items that best meet the employees’ relocation needs.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.7 The amount of money available in an employee’s Core Customized Fund is

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.7 The amount of money available in an employee’s Core Customized Fund is] determined by the funding formula (see Appendix B) and is different for each employee based upon his/her personal circumstances. Since this fund is for the sole purpose of enhancing a move, unused or remaining monies shall be returned to the department and are not payable to the employee.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.8 The Core Customized Fund cannot be used to supplement expenses covered by
metadata.subsection: TDRA inclusive of rent in advance

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.8 The Core Customized Fund cannot be used to supplement expenses covered by | TDRA inclusive of rent in advance] the Personalized Fund. 3.3.9 For information on the conditions and limitations of a particular entitlement, refer to the appropriate section within this directive. The following is an overview of entitlements available to relocating employees under the Core Customized Fund: Interim accommodations, meals and miscellaneous relocation allowance

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.10 Calculation of the Core Customized funding shall be determined from the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.10 Calculation of the Core Customized funding shall be determined from the] chart found in Appendix B. For more detailed information on the conditions and limitations on any part of the calculation, please refer to the appropriate area within this section. Real Estate Commission - The first element used to calculate the Core Customized Fund varies based upon the accommodation status at the time of relocation notification as follows: (a) Homeowner: the greater of $1000 or 35% of the real estate commission payable based upon the established appraised value of the home to a maximum of $5,250. (applicable taxes excluded); (i) Renter: A renter at origin will be credited with $1,000;(ii) The sale price of the principal residence will be used in lieu of an appraisal to establish the funding envelope should an employee’s principal residence be sold within the time that a letter of offer has been received and before the consultative process commences with the CRSP. (iii) Transportation of Family - The second element used to calculate the Core Customized Fund is the cost of one-way transportation to the location of the new workplace. (b) For calculation purposes only, the funding will be based on the distance, one-way, between the former and new workplace, multiplied by the family size, multiplied by the appropriate kilometric rate as per the Department of Finance annual rate (rate based on point of origin), and multiplied by 35%. (i) In those instances where the employee and dependants are transported to the new destination via the employer’s owned/leased transportation, employees will not be entitled to this element of the Core Customized Fund and the mileage calculation from the origin post to the new post will not apply. (ii) Transportation of Household Goods - The third element used to calculate the Core Customized Fund is based upon the cost of shipping an average of 1,000 lb/453.60 kg household goods per unit/room from one location to another. Household goods exclude vehicles of any kind. (c)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.11 Spousal Services - The following services provided to the employee’s spouse or

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.3.11 Spousal Services - The following services provided to the employee’s spouse or] common-law partner may be reimbursed from the Core Customized Fund: 3.3.12 Expenses are subject to CRA policy and guidelines. 3.3.13 Receipts are required. 3.3.14 A taxable benefit could result from these reimbursements.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4 Personalized Fund

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4 Personalized Fund | Purpose] Qualifying Rooms Include: kitchen, bedrooms (including bedrooms in finished basement), living room, recreation room, family room, dining room, basement, garage (not condos and apartments), out- building/storage shed (limit of one), storage room (separate from apartment) confirmed via appraisals for home owners and a signed listing for renters. (i) An employee sharing accommodations is entitled to shipment of HG&E weight factor based on the rental formula. Example: 3 people rent a 3- bedroom house and rent is based on “the/a room”. The employee is entitled to the weight factor of one (1) room. If the rental formula is based on the entire premises divisible by the number (3) of shared tenants, then the employee is entitled to 1/3 of the entire number of rooms. (ii) This applies to articles shipped under the Core Fund that the moving firm will accept on a straight-weight basis only (policy centre confirmed rates). (iii) In those exceptional cases where labourers are flown into remote communities for packing, etc., this cost will be excluded from the formula configuration being used. (iv) employment search;(a) employment assistance;(b) travel to/from interviews;(c) preparation of CV; and(d) photocopy and transmittal costs for transcripts of academic records.(e)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.1 The amount of money in the Personalized Fund is generated from

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.1 The amount of money in the Personalized Fund is generated from] savings/incentives and allowances as described in subsection 3.4.5.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.2 Reasonable and justifiable expenses exceeding the Core Fund limitations, which

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.2 Reasonable and justifiable expenses exceeding the Core Fund limitations, which] fall within the intent of this directive and are supported by original receipts, may also be claimed from the Personalized Fund.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.3 The employee will have the final decision on how the Personalized Fund is to be

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.3 The employee will have the final decision on how the Personalized Fund is to be] expended; however, amounts that exceed third-party rates/fees (e.g. real estate commissions, legal fees, inspection fees, etc.) will not be reimbursed. 3.4.4 Upon completion of the move or at the end of one year, whichever comes first, any remaining monies from the Personalized Fund will be paid out to the employee. It should be noted that any pay out from the Personalized Fund is taxable.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.5 Calculation of the Personalized Fund – The calculation of the Personalized

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.5 Calculation of the Personalized Fund – The calculation of the Personalized] funding formula shall be determined from the chart in Appendix C. For more detailed information on the conditions and limitations on any part of the calculation, please refer to the appropriate area within this section.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.6 Transferable Savings to Personalized Fund - Employees may increase their
metadata.subsection: Stay over must be at destination, not en route;(ii)

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.6 Transferable Savings to Personalized Fund - Employees may increase their | Stay over must be at destination, not en route;(ii)] Personalized Fund by realizing savings in the following ways: Non-Accountable Incidental Expenses Allowance - Employees shall receive the Non-Accountable Incidental Expenses Allowance credited to the Personalized Fund in an amount of $650. This allowance shall be paid 30 days in advance of the move date of the HG&E. (a) Transfer Allowances - Employees shall receive a Transfer Allowance equivalent to two (2) weeks salary. The allowance is based on the annual salary effective on the date of appointment at the new location and shall be paid 30 days in advance of the move date of the HG&E. (b) Real Estate Commission Savings - Employees who elect not to sell their homes at their former workplace may transfer 80% of the real estate commission fees that would have been payable had the home been sold (taxes excluded) to the Personalized Fund; (a) the employee must exercise this option within fifteen working days of receiving the first appraisal report. For greater clarity, subsequent (i) appraisals, if any, do not extend the elect not to sell period; the amount payable is calculated on the appraised value at pre-negotiated real estate commission rates, capped at $12,000; (ii) those taking this credit must sign a waiver foregoing any future reimbursement by the Crown of real estate fees, legal fees or other related disposal costs for the property in question; and (iii) Savings from a Shorter House Hunting Trip (HHT) - Employees authorized to drive 650 km or less one-way and who have successfully completed the HHT in less than 5 days may transfer 100% of all lodging, meals, and incidental savings for the unused days (capped at $250) to the Personalized Fund. A normal length HHT consists of the following: (b) 5 days meals and incidental allowance (HHT) + 2 days (travel status);(i) 5 nights accommodation (HHT) + 1 night accommodation (travel status);(ii) for example, an employee and spouse or common-law partner departed on Sunday and returned on Wednesday used 3 days meals and 3 nights accommodation. The employee could transfer the cost of 2 days meals for each of the employee and the spouse or common-law partner and 2 nights accommodation and 2 days incidentals up to a maximum of $250 to the (iii) Incentive for Staying over a Saturday Night - Employees on HHT who fly and arrange their itinerary so that they save the department airfare costs by staying over a Saturday

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.6 Transferable Savings to Personalized Fund - Employees may increase their
metadata.subsection: Stay over must be at destination, not en route;(ii)

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 3.4.6 Transferable Savings to Personalized Fund - Employees may increase their | Stay over must be at destination, not en route;(ii)] and returned on Wednesday used 3 days meals and 3 nights accommodation. The employee could transfer the cost of 2 days meals for each of the employee and the spouse or common-law partner and 2 nights accommodation and 2 days incidentals up to a maximum of $250 to the (iii) Incentive for Staying over a Saturday Night - Employees on HHT who fly and arrange their itinerary so that they save the department airfare costs by staying over a Saturday night at destination shall receive $250 into their Personalized Fund. (c) Savings are based upon return full fare economy airfare from point of origin to final destination, direct/non-stop (except when the carrier forces a connection/stopover such as flight from Halifax to Whitehorse); (i) Does not apply to employees relocating into Crown homes or facilities;(iii) Hotel-Motel Room Reduction Savings/ Incentive - An employee who uses less than his or her hotel room entitlement (see section 4.9), based on family size, shall receive a flat rate incentive of $50.00 per night which will be transferred to the Personalized Fund; (d)

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.1 The House Hunting Trip (HHT) is meant to provide an employee with the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.1 The House Hunting Trip (HHT) is meant to provide an employee with the] opportunity to secure suitable accommodation at the workplace.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.2 The HHT should result in most moves being door-to-door, hence considerably

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.2 The HHT should result in most moves being door-to-door, hence considerably] reducing the cost for interim accommodation, meals, and incidental expenses and eliminating unnecessary storage in transit costs.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.3 Employees who plan to re-occupy a previously occupied residence, or who have

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.1.3 Employees who plan to re-occupy a previously occupied residence, or who have] already secured accommodation, or who have been assigned an “Official” Crown- owned or leased accommodation associated to special functions, are not entitled to a HHT. However, entitlement exists for a destination home inspection trip (DHIT).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.2.1 Before proceeding on an HHT/DHIT, prior approval must be received from the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.2.1 Before proceeding on an HHT/DHIT, prior approval must be received from the] manager. An employee who is relocating to a new post (including Isolated Posts) where a viable market exists is eligible for an HHT. Employees are deemed to be on travel status for the normal duration of the HHT (5+2 days). Annual leave/ compensatory time off may be used when the HHT is extended beyond the normal Transportation of Household Goods Below the Pre-move Estimate - When an employee reduces the amount of HG&E being shipped under the core envelope to the new location they qualify for the Transportation of Household Goods Below the Pre-move Estimate incentive. The incentive is based on the difference between the CRS pre-move estimate conducted by the van lines prior to the move and the actual weight of goods which are shipped. The employee whose entire shipment of HG&E is below the established pre-move estimate shall have 80% of the savings resulting from the lower amount shipped transferred to their Personalized Fund. This entitlement is subject to the following: (e) the shipment of vehicles are not included for calculation purposes; and(i) employees who are proceeding unaccompanied are not entitled to this benefit. (ii) duration. Reimbursement of allowable HHT expenses shall be paid as if the employee or spouse or common-law partner, or both, were in travel status in accordance with the relevant module of the NJC Travel Directive. Only one incidental expense shall be paid per family.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.3.1 Employees may schedule their HHT in order to benefit from provisions that

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.3.1 Employees may schedule their HHT in order to benefit from provisions that] enable them to transfer funds into their Personalized Fund. See paragraphs 3.4.6(b) and (c).

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 4.4.1 Total travel time shall not exceed two (2) days, except when distances and/or

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.4.1 Total travel time shall not exceed two (2) days, except when distances and/or] connections are such that the total return journey cannot be accomplished in that period. In such cases, additional travel time shall be authorized by the Departmental National Coordinator and those expenses shall be paid from the Core Fund.

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 4.4.2 However, additional travel time and related expenses, resulting from the] employee selecting a different mode of transportation other than that which would normally have been used, will be paid from the Personalized Fund.

The total duration of the HHT shall not normally exceed 7 days (6 nights) total, inclusive of travel time.

(b) family size of 1 = 1 room for 1 person;(a) family size of 2 = 1 room;(b) family size of 3 to 5 = 2 rooms;(c) family size of 6 or 7 = 3 rooms;(d) family size of 8 or more = 4 rooms.(e)

Expenses for childcare and a car rental may also be reimbursed if necessary. However, the 15 days of IAM&MA shall not normally be extended. Any extensions to IAM&MA shall be administered in accordance with Part V.

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 5.1.1 The payment of IAM&MA either at the point of origin and/or at destination is] meant to reimburse employees for living costs incurred when they cannot occupy their own living accommodation because:

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 5.2.1 Employees shall attempt to coordinate the move of HG&E as closely as possible] with their reporting date/the disposal of their old residence, and the occupancy of their new residence in order to minimize the time spent in interim accommodation.

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 5.2.2 Managers shall allow flexibility of reporting dates to enable the employee to] coordinate relocation activities effectively and in the most cost-effective manner.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 11.13.1 The costs to ship recreational vehicles, such as boats, motorcycles, all-terrain

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 11.13.1 The costs to ship recreational vehicles, such as boats, motorcycles, all-terrain] vehicles, trailers, and snowmobiles shall be reimbursed from the Core Customized Fund.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 11.14.1 Employees are responsible for any additional costs beyond those established

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 11.14.1 Employees are responsible for any additional costs beyond those established] in the HGRS contract. Employees wishing to ship items that fall outside the scope of the contract shall be reimbursed for actual expenses from the Core Customized Fund.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 11.15.1 Employees shall be reimbursed from the Core Customized Fund, actual and

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 11.15.1 Employees shall be reimbursed from the Core Customized Fund, actual and] reasonable expenses for transportation of their pets to the new location and any necessary kennel fees incurred while the employees are in interim accommodation.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 11.16.1 An employee shall be provided up to three (3) days with pay to supervise the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 11.16.1 An employee shall be provided up to three (3) days with pay to supervise the] packing and loading of personal effects for shipment to the new location.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 11.17.1 An employee shall be provided up to two (2) days with pay to supervise the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 11.17.1 An employee shall be provided up to two (2) days with pay to supervise the] unpacking and unloading of personal effects at the new location.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.1 The intention behind employee-requested relocations is for employers to

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.1 The intention behind employee-requested relocations is for employers to] recognize that there may be circumstances in employees’ personal lives that may lead them to ask for employment in another location, such as a death or illness in the family. Employers can respond to these requests on a compassionate basis by authorizing a relocation, when it is possible for them to do so. The intent of authorizing an employee-requested relocation on compassionate grounds is not to avoid the costs associated with an employer-requested relocation that is a part of the normal staffing activities of an employer.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.2 An employee may request an employee-requested relocation for personal or

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.2 An employee may request an employee-requested relocation for personal or] compassionate reasons. DNCs may approve an employee-requested relocation when: 12.1.3 The Departmental National Coordinator shall ensure that:

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.4 When an employee-requested relocation is approved, the employee is entitled] to:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.5 Employees may claim reasonable and actual relocation expenses listed in this

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.5 Employees may claim reasonable and actual relocation expenses listed in this] directive until the Core Customized Fund is depleted. There is no assistance for disposal or acquisition of a principal residence, including rental related expenses.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.6 Employees may claim a Non-Accountable Incidental Expense Allowance in the

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.6 Employees may claim a Non-Accountable Incidental Expense Allowance in the] amount of $650 as part of the $5,000.00 allocation of Core Customized fund as there is a vacant position at the appropriate group and level at destination;(a) the deputy head or senior delegated officer provides written certification that the relocation meets the intent as described in subsection 12.1.1; and (b) the employee acknowledges in writing that the relocation is employee- requested and that the benefits are limited to those outlined in this section. (c) employees are provided with counselling and written confirmation on the applicable provisions of this directive from the CRSP; and (a) copies of all correspondence are retained on the employee’s relocation file.(b) the sum of up to five thousand dollars ($5,000.00) in their Core Customized Fund; (a) the Basic Core and Personalized Funds do not apply;(b) unused or remaining monies shall be returned to the Receiver General of Canada/department and are not payable to the employee as a cash-payout; and (c) a contract with a relocation services supplier who will provide the employee with professional assistance such as counselling on the relocation benefits available, guidance on accommodation at the new location and expense management. (d) follows:

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.7 All commercial travel arrangements are to be made through the Government’s

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 12.1.7 All commercial travel arrangements are to be made through the Government’s] contracted travel services. Employees are governed by the NJC Travel Directive.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.1.1 Employees authorized under the Treasury Board Telework Policy to work from

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.1.1 Employees authorized under the Treasury Board Telework Policy to work from] their current place of residence in Canada instead of being relocated to the new workplace shall be subject to this directive upon termination of the Telework arrangement.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.2.1 The provisions of the Isolated Posts and Government Housing Directive

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.2.1 The provisions of the Isolated Posts and Government Housing Directive] (IPGHD) related to relocation take precedence over this directive.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.3 Unaccompanied Moves within Canada

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.3 Unaccompanied Moves within Canada] 13.3.1 The purpose of this provision is to provide greater flexibility for families who, for employment, education, or family-related reasons, may not wish to relocate to the employee’s new workplace.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.1 Relocation duration in excess of three (3) years is considered a permanent

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.1 Relocation duration in excess of three (3) years is considered a permanent] relocation.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.2 An employee who opts to have the family remain behind permanently while

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.2 An employee who opts to have the family remain behind permanently while] he/she proceeds to the new destination alone may opt to receive the Real Estate receipts are not required however they should be retained by the employee in the event of a tax audit; (a) the employee must sign a statement certifying that the expenses were incurred.(b) Commission Savings incentive at paragraph 3.4.6(a). The CRSP will withhold 10% until conclusion of the relocation. 13.4.3 The employee is personally responsible for all costs incurred for family visits.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.3 If the employee’s workplace changes again, involving a second relocation to a

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.4.3 If the employee’s workplace changes again, involving a second relocation to a] third workplace, the entitlements to relocation benefits for members of the family, who were not relocated previously, shall not exceed relocation costs from the location where the employee resides had the family been residing with the employee.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.5.1 This directive may apply to employees on assignments for more than

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.5.1 This directive may apply to employees on assignments for more than] one (1) year’s duration, by mutual agreement of the employer and employee. Where the assignment is for more than one (1) year and less than three (3) years, the provisions on sale (see Part VIII) and purchase of property (see Part IX) will not apply.

metadata.section: NJC Relocation Directive: 13.5.2 Departments and employees should exercise care when contemplating

content: [NJC Relocation Directive | NJC Relocation Directive: 13.5.2 Departments and employees should exercise care when contemplating] relocation to the assignment location as no further relocation benefits will be provided if the assignment is extended or becomes permanent as the employee will already have relocated to the new workplace.
